domain: Karelian peatland bog body preservation (4-axis chemistry: anaerobic + acidic tannins + cold + sphagnan-N-sequestration; soft tissue preserved over millennia without active intervention; bone selectively dissolves)
specific_mechanism: Multi-axis passive chemical preservation stack with no active maintenance; selective preservation (soft tissue protected, hard tissue partially dissolved); reversible upon removal from preservation environment.
llm_application: BOG-PRESERVE: a multi-axis continual-fine-tuning regularizer stack applied to a SELECT subset of base-model parameters (the 'soft tissue' = critical safety/identity layers): (i) anaerobic-style ZERO-GRADIENT MASKING (no aerobic decay = no gradient flow); (ii) tannin-style L2/L_inf BINDING to current values; (iii) cold-style locally-VANISHING LR; (iv) sphagnan-style MOMENTUM DECOUPLING. The other 'hard tissue' parameters (peripheral layers) are allowed to drift normally (analog: bone dissolves). All four mechanisms operate PASSIVELY without active gating; reversible by removing the regularizer stack.
novelty_claim: EWC, LwF, SI use weight importance penalties (one-axis). LoRA freezes base + trains adapters. Gradient-Mask Tuning masks by gradient magnitude (one-axis). DS-Init/DeepNet/Peri-LN are init/norm-based. CPL/REGAL/forgetting-curve replay are replay-based. BOG-PRESERVE differs by 4-MECHANISM STACK (zero-gradient mask + L2-binding + locally-vanishing LR + momentum decoupling) applied PASSIVELY to a SELECT critical-layer subset, with peripheral parameters intentionally allowed to drift.
